title: Add auxiliary time-to-stop targets, not just next-lap labels
summary: Pit-now is the sharp edge of a broader time-to-stop process.
rationale: Pit-now is the sharp edge of a broader time-to-stop process. Recent pit-stop work and event-forecasting papers report gains from jointly modeling immediate classification with remaining horizon, which should help separate true negatives from rows that are simply 2-5 laps early.
implementation_hint: Create fold-safe auxiliary targets such as `pit_within_2`, `pit_within_3`, `pit_within_5`, and/or `laps_until_next_pit` inside each driver-race sequence after splitting by race groups. Train shared or specialist models, then feed only OOF auxiliary predictions into the final `PitNextLap` model.
expected_effect: Better ranking on borderline laps near the pit window; fewer false negatives just before actual stops.
risk: Easy leakage trap if horizons are built before fold splitting or if future labels from held-out races are touched.
